name: Piper TTS
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); fast local TTS; from Rhasspy home automation project

auth.notes: Local inference; ONNX model files downloaded separately

performance.notes: Real-time factor >100x on CPU — extremely fast; ONNX runtime, no GPU needed

agent_readiness.notes: Ultra-fast local TTS running on CPU via ONNX. 40+ voices across 20+ languages. MIT license. Very small models (~50MB). Real-time factor >100x — much faster than Kokoro/Coqui on CPU. No GPU needed. Used in Rhasspy voice assistant and Home Assistant. Streaming support. Command-line and Python API. Best for embedded/edge/IoT TTS where speed and small footprint matter most.

gotchas: Voice quality good but below Kokoro or ElevenLabs
Fixed voice set — no voice cloning
Less natural prosody than larger models
best_when: Edge/embedded/IoT TTS on CPU (Raspberry Pi, etc.)
Home automation voice synthesis (Home Assistant integration)
Ultra-low latency TTS for real-time applications
When model size and CPU performance matter most
avoid_when: When Kokoro's higher quality is preferred (also runs on CPU)
summary: Piper is an ultra-fast CPU TTS engine (100x+ real-time factor) using ONNX models. 40+ voices, 20+ languages, ~50MB model size. MIT license. No GPU needed. Used in Home Assistant and Rhasspy voice assistant. Best for edge/embedded TTS where speed and small footprint matter most. Voice quality good but below Kokoro or ElevenLabs.
